The '''''Majestic Gundark''''' was a [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]] with only slight modifications, including an upper and a lower turret. It was used by the eco-terrorist group [[Army of Life]].<ref name="OC" />

Army agent [[Del Binjitt]] piloted the ''Gundark'' to [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. The ''Gundark'' was then loaded with dangerous bio-material ([[clodhopper]] eggs) while Binjitt found independent agents to pilot the ship to [[Stend IV]]. The bio-material would devastate [[Gran/Legends|Gran]] colonies of Stend IV, should it arrive there. Meanwhile, the ship was left on docking bay 33 of [[Kwilaan/Legends|Kwilaan]] spaceport.<ref name="OC" />

The independent agents reached docking bay 33, but they were being followed by port authorities. They had to fight their way to the ship, and then take off.<ref name="OC" />

However, they could not abandon Naboo before dealing with lieutenant [[Davonn]] and his partner in two [[N-1]] fighters. The ''Gundark'' reached hyperspace, but Davonn managed to track its vector and reported to his superiors.<ref name="OC" />

The ''Gundark'' then traveled to Stend IV, but the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] had been informed and they had sent a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], [[Dree-Tekes]], who reached Stend IV before the ''Gundark'' did. Dree-Tekes recruited four local Gran militiamen to fight the ''Gundark'' and its crew when it arrived; they were provided with blaster carbines, a heavy blaster cannon and a grenade launcher.<ref name="OC" />

The ''Gundark'' reached the biggest settlement on Stend IV, [[Gev-Toosa]], but Dree-Tekes and her men were waiting for her and [[Showdown on Stend IV|attacked]] the ship with the grenade launcher. The crew then tried to land and strike back.<ref name="OC" />

{{Ship-stub}}
